title: Ic device authentication using energy characterization
abstract: Systems, methods, and apparatuses are described for verifying the authenticity of an integrated circuit device. An integrated test apparatus may use quiescent current and/or conducted electromagnetic interference readings to determine if a device under test matches the characteristics of an authenticated device. Deviations from the characteristics of the authenticated device may be indicative of a counterfeit device.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A method comprising: applying a first voltage to an unauthenticated integrated circuit (1C) device; determining, using the first voltage, a first quiescent current (QC) reading for the unauthenticated 1C device; determining, based on the first QC reading, a tested QC value; determining, based at least in part on a difference between the tested QC value and an expected QC value, that a threshold is not satisfied, wherein the expected QC value is associated with an authenticated 1C device; and outputting an indication that the threshold is not satisfied. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein applying the first voltage comprises applying the first voltage across a Vcc connection and a GND connection of the unauthenticated IC device. 3. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: applying a plurality of incremental voltages to the unauthenticated IC device; and determining, for each of the incremental voltages, a corresponding QC value, wherein the determining that the threshold is not satisfied is further based on differences between the corresponding QC values and additional expected QC values associated with the authenticated IC device. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the determining that the threshold is not satisfied comprises determining that the threshold is not satisfied based on an r- squared value, and further comprising determining the r-squared value based on the corresponding QC values. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 - 4, further comprising: applying a steady-state voltage to the unauthenticated IC device; 

 applying, while applying the steady-state voltage, an excitation signal to an input of the unauthenticated 1C device; determining, based on the applied steady-state voltage and the applied excitation signal, a tested conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) value of the unauthenticated 1C device; and determining, based on a difference between the tested conducted EMI value and an expected conducted EMI value, that a second threshold is not satisfied, wherein the expected conducted EMI value is associated with the authenticated 1C device, and wherein the outputting further comprises outputting a second indication that the second threshold is not satisfied. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising determining that the unauthenticated 1C device is a counterfeit device based on determining that both the threshold and the second threshold are not satisfied. 7. A method comprising: applying a steady-state voltage to an unauthenticated integrated circuit (1C) device; applying, while applying the steady-state voltage, an excitation signal to an input of the unauthenticated 1C device; determining, based on the applied steady-state voltage and the applied excitation signal, a tested conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) value of the unauthenticated 1C device; and determining, based on a difference between the tested conducted EMI value and an expected conducted EMI value, that a threshold is not satisfied, wherein expected conducted EMI value is associated with an authenticated 1C device; and outputting an indication that the threshold is not satisfied. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the determining the tested conducted EMI value comprises detecting a high frequency signal on a Vcc connection of the unauthenticated 1C device. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the detecting the high frequency signal on the Vcc connection comprises detecting a high frequency signal output from a high pass filter on the Vcc connection. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the determining the tested conducted EMI value comprises performing lossy integration, based on the high frequency signal, to determine the tested conducted EMI value. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the determining the tested conducted EMI value comprises performing a squaring operation, based on the high frequency signal, to determine the tested conducted EMI value. 12. The method of any one of claims 7 - 1 1 , further comprising: determining a first quiescent current (QC) reading for the unauthenticated IC device; determining, based on the first QC reading, a tested QC value; and determining, based at least in part on a difference between the tested QC value and an expected QC value, that a second threshold is not satisfied, wherein the expected QC value is associated with the authenticated IC device, and wherein the outputting further comprises outputting a second indication that the second threshold is not satisfied. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising determining that the unauthenticated IC device is a counterfeit device based on determining that both the threshold and the second threshold are not satisfied. 14. A method comprising: determining, based at least in part on a difference between a tested quiescent current (QC) value and an expected QC value, that a first threshold is not satisfied, wherein the tested QC value is associated with an unauthenticated integrated circuit 

 (IC) device, and wherein the expected QC value is associated with an authenticated IC device; determining, based on a difference between a tested conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) value and an expected conducted EMI value, that a second threshold is not satisfied, wherein the tested conducted EMI value is associated with the unauthenticated 1C device, and wherein the expected conducted EMI value is associated with the authenticated 1C device; and outputting based on determining that at least one threshold of the first threshold or the second threshold is not satisfied, an indication that the at least one threshold is not satisfied. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising: applying a first voltage to the unauthenticated IC device; determining, using the first voltage, a first QC reading for the unauthenticated IC device; and determining, based on the first QC reading, the tested QC value. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising: applying a steady-state voltage to the unauthenticated IC device; applying, while applying the steady-state voltage, an excitation signal to an input of the unauthenticated IC device; and determining, based on the applied steady-state voltage and the applied excitation signal, the tested conducted EMI value of the unauthenticated IC device. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein applying the first voltage comprises applying the first voltage across a Vcc connection and a GND connection of the unauthenticated IC device. 18. The method of claim 16, further comprising: applying a plurality of incremental voltages to the unauthenticated IC device; and 

 determining, for each of the incremental voltages, a corresponding QC value, wherein the determining that the first threshold is not satisfied is further based on differences between the corresponding QC values and additional expected QC values associated with the authenticated IC device. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the determining that the first threshold is not satisfied comprises determining that the first threshold is not satisfied based on an r- squared value, and further comprising determining the r-squared value based on the corresponding QC values. 20. The method of any one of claims 16 - 19, wherein the determining the tested conducted EMI value comprises detecting a high frequency signal on a Vcc connection of the unauthenticated IC device. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the determining the tested conducted EMI value comprises performing lossy integration, based on the high frequency signal, to determine the tested conducted EMI value.
